content.problemStatement:
You are given an unweighted tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). Let's denote the distance between any two nodes ppp and qqq by d(p,q)d(p, q)d(p,q).

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given parameters aaa, dad_ada​, bbb, dbd_bdb​, and you should find a node xxx such that d(x,a)=dad(x, a) = d_ad(x,a)=da​ and d(x,b)=dbd(x, b) = d_bd(x,b)=db​, or determine that there is no such node.
constraints: the graph on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains four space-separated integers aaa, dad_ada​, bbb and dbd_bdb​ describing a query.
content.outputFormat:
For each query, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of a node satisfying the given requirements, or −1-1−1 if no such node exists.

If there are multiple solutions, you may output any one.
